intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The visual data indicates a critical failure of the expected biome registry. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visible as living specimens. Only P3 (Pothos) and its associated scale anchor (white rabbit) are present in the frame.

inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected in black pot.

inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected in black pot.

plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface; no signs of growth.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Dark, appears consistently moist or saturated across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: White, crystalline-like debris (possibly perlite or mineral salts) present on the surface of P3.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: Over the 5-day observation period, there has been no visible recovery or growth. The state of the pots has remained static, suggesting either complete plant loss or extreme dormancy/failure to thrive.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of 'Systemic Collapse'. The lack of chlorophyll-bearing tissue in P1, P2, and P4 indicates these specimens are dead. P3 shows no signs of active growth or vitality.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a white, cylindrical object (likely a sensor or probe) in the yellow pot (P1) is a new intervention not fully detailed in the initial registry.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is currently non-viable. The pots are largely devoid of active plant life, with the exception of the P3 container which holds the scale anchor. The soil appears dark and potentially waterlogged, which may have contributed to the loss of the other specimens. No new growth or recovery has been observed throughout the chronological sequence.
